serviceName: Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka (MSK)
serviceDescription: Amazon MSK is a fully managed service that makes it easy for you to build and run applications that use Apache Kafka to process streaming data. With Amazon MSK, you can use native Apache Kafka APIs to populate data lakes, stream changes to and from databases, and power machine learning and analytics applications.

cards.title: Broker instances
cards.description: Instance usage by default is accounted for a month
cards.helpText: To learn about right-sizing your MSK cluster, see the cluster sizing tool in our best practices :

cards.title: (Optional) Provisioned Storage Throughput
cards.helpText: For a simple real world example of the throughput component, see the Fsx for windows file server calculator. Read more about the throughput input component in Wiki.

cards.title: (Optional) Multi-VPC Private Connectivity
cards.helpText: Multi-VPC private connectivity allows you to easily connect Kafka clients hosted in different Virtual Private Clouds (VPCs) and AWS accounts to an Amazon MSK cluster.

INTRA_REGION.title: Intra-Region Data Transfer
INTRA_REGION.description: Enter the data you expect to transfer between Availability Zones in [region]. Note: Replication across Availability Zones is included in the cost of Amazon MSK.

cards.title: MSK Serverless
cards.helpText: With MSK Serverless, you pay an hourly rate for your serverless clusters and an hourly rate for each partition that you create. Additionally, you pay per GB of data that your producers write to and your consumers read from the topics in your cluster. Amazon MSK charges you only for the storage you consume.

components.label: pricing for MSK Serverless Ingress Throughput
pricing for MSK Serverless Egress Throughput

pricing for MSK Serverless Egress Throughput

cards.title: MSK Connect
cards.helpText: When you setup a Kafka Connect connector with Amazon MSK Connect, you pay for the time your connector runs and standard data transfer fees for data in and out of your connector.
